reports.thesis: long/short ratio 3.1 = longs heavily crowded; funding +0.04% = longs paying dearly = flush risk; OI +9% into a failing high = late longs stacked above a thin shelf (liquidation fuel).
long/short ratio 0.72 = crowd short; funding -0.03% pays the long; OI +6% as price holds = new shorts trapped, squeeze fuel.
